title: The Watch On The Kerb
content:
Night-Lights are falling;
    Girl of the street,
    Go to your calling
    If you would eat.
    Lamplight and starlight
    And moonlight superb,
    Bright hope is a farlight,
    So watch on the kerb.
    Watch on the kerb,
    Watch on the kerb;
    Hope is a farlight;
    Then watch on the kerb.

    Comes a man: call him,
    Gone! he is vext;
    Curses befall him,
    Wait for the next!
    Fair world and bright world,
    Life still is sweet,
    Girl of the night-world,
    Watch on the street.

    Dreary the watch is:
    Moon sinks from sight,
    Gas only blotches
    Darkness with light;
    Never, Oh, never
    Let courage go down;
    Keep from the river,
    Oh, Girl of the Town!
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Henry Lawson delivers a powerful performance in "The Watch On The Kerb"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
